{{Quote|You are fighting the wrong enemy, Sylvar. The anger is within you. You must kill it…but beware, for it is dangerous prey.|Kharr|<ref name="redemption3" />}}
'''Kharr''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] holy man who was a leader of his [[city-tree]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Cathar (location)/Legends|Cathar]]. Kharr was a mentor to [[Sylvar]], who went on to become a famous and celebrated [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Kharr was extremely proud of his protégé, and he led a large parade in her honor when she returned home in [[3986 BBY]]. However, Sylvar was not there to celebrate, as she met with Kharr later that night in order to discuss how to regain the inner peace that had eluded her since the [[Death/Legends|death]] of her mate, [[Crado]]. Kharr advised her to take a proactive approach in dispelling the simmering anger that he could sense within her, leading Sylvar to go on a ritual [[Blood Hunt/Legends|blood hunt]] in order to achieve this.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I can sense the storm in your thoughts, and in your heart. You have come here to regain your peace…but you don't know where to begin your search.|Kharr, to Sylvar|<ref name="redemption3" />}}
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]], Kharr was a holy man who held a leadership position among the natives of his [[city-tree]] on [[Cathar (location)/Legends|Cathar]], his [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Despite his lofty status, however, Kharr lived in a simple tent on one of the [[Tree/Legends|tree's]] large branches.<ref name="redemption3" /> In his younger days, Kharr mentored a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Cathar named [[Sylvar]],<ref name="redemption4">[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 4|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 4]]</ref> who went on to become a famous [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and his [[City/Legends|city]]-tree's most beloved and celebrated denizen; the two held a bond that would persist for [[Standard year/Legends|years]].<ref name="redemption3" />

In [[3986 BBY]], Sylvar, trying to cleanse herself of simmering anger resulting from the [[Death/Legends|death]] of her mate, [[Crado]], [[3996 BBY|ten years before]], returned home. She received a hero's welcome, and Kharr was the first to greet her as she approached their city-tree. Although Sylvar was reluctant, Kharr accompanied her and her traveling companion, [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Tott Doneeta]], on a parade in her honor. That night, Kharr met with Sylvar by his tent, sensing that she was grappling with herself and lacked inner peace. Kharr suggested to her that she should find a way to rid herself of the anger that raged inside her—taking his advice to [[Heart/Legends|heart]], Sylvar decided to go on a ritual [[Blood Hunt/Legends|blood hunt]], hoping to rid herself of her unwanted fury. However, the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] failed to restore Sylvar's inner tranquility.<ref name="redemption3" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Thank you for seeing me, Kharr. You always understand…|Sylvar|<ref name="redemption3" />}}
Kharr was extremely proud of Sylvar and her accomplishments as a famed Jedi, and he was overjoyed when she returned home in 3986 BBY. He could sense when she was distressed—Sylvar remarked that he always understood her. Kharr deeply cared for Sylvar and was always willing to help her in troubled [[Time/Legends|times]]. The Cathar holy man had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] covering his whole body and often wore a red robe and [[Hat/Legends|hat]].<ref name="redemption3" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kharr made his first and only appearance in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in the [[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 3|third issue]] of [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Redemption|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Redemption'']], the final story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1998]]. In this appearance, Kharr was illustrated by [[Chris Gossett]].<ref name="redemption3" />

*[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 3]] {{1st}}
